findings.title: check-gate-2-exit.sh has no behavioral test coverage
findings.why_it_matters: CHECK-2 is the critical exit gate from code to commit. The only test runs --help (or no args) and merely asserts the script produces non-empty output. None of the compliance parsing, BLOCK/WARN counting, VERIFICATION.md generation, or interactive prompt branches are exercised. A regression in gate logic would not be caught.
evidence: Assertion is only: if [ -n "\$output" ]; then PASS
No tests for BLOCK>0, missing compliance-review-report.md, compile confirm no, or VERIFICATION.md decision validation

findings.title: No CI workflow runs the new shell integration tests
findings.why_it_matters: The old validate.yml (which ran npm test) was deleted, and no replacement workflow was added to execute tests/integration/*.sh. The new integration tests exist on disk but have no automated execution path, so regressions will only be discovered manually.
evidence: diff shows validate.yml deleted with no replacement workflow added
package.json deleted; no npm test runner remains
New tests are shell scripts (test-init.sh, test-feature.sh, test-code.sh, test-full-flow.sh) with no CI invocation

findings.title: test-full-flow.sh omits tech-code and tech-commit SKILL.md checks
findings.why_it_matters: The test claims to validate the full init -> feature -> code -> commit flow, but test_skills_exist only asserts tech-init and tech-feature SKILL.md exist. Missing assertions for tech-code and tech-commit mean broken or missing skill files in the code/commit phases would pass the full-flow test.
evidence: skills/tech-code/SKILL.md and skills/tech-commit/SKILL.md are not in the array

findings.title: check-gate-2-enter.sh missing SPEC-STATE and malformed-doc edge cases
findings.why_it_matters: The gate script has branches for SPEC-STATE != PLAN, spec.md with 0 decisions, and tasks.md with 0 tasks. Only the 'empty directory fail' and 'complete docs pass' cases are tested. A regression in the SPEC-STATE branch or decision/task counting logic would go unnoticed.
evidence: check-gate-2-enter.sh lines 29876-29892: checks CURRENT_STATE = PLAN with WARN fallback
test-code.sh test_check_gate_2_enter only tests empty dir fail and sample-feature pass
No test for spec.md present but with 0 D-XXX decisions or tasks.md present but with 0 T-XXX tasks

findings.title: pattern-scan.sh 'not found' branches are untested
findings.why_it_matters: pattern-scan.sh has early-return branches for when no Controller, Service, Repository, Entity, Config, or Exception patterns are found. The fixture always has these patterns, so only the 'found' paths are exercised. A refactor breaking the empty-project handling would not be caught.
evidence: scan_controllers: if [ "\$controller_count" -eq 0 ]; then echo '未检测到 Controller 类'; return; fi
sample-java-project fixture contains UserController, UserService, UserRepository, User entity
No test runs pattern-scan.sh against an empty or non-Java project directory

findings.title: detect-stack.sh missing dual-build-tool and FORCE_BUILD_TOOL tests
findings.why_it_matters: The script documents Maven priority when both pom.xml and build.gradle exist, and mentions a FORCE_BUILD_TOOL override. These branches are not exercised. A regression in priority logic or environment handling would silently change stack detection results.
evidence: detect-stack.sh comment: '检测优先级：Maven > Gradle' and '如需强制指定，可设置 FORCE_BUILD_TOOL 环境变量覆盖'
test-init.sh tests Maven-only, Gradle-only, Kotlin-DSL-only, and no-build-tool cases
No test creates both pom.xml and build.gradle in the same directory

residual_risks: The deleted JS tests covered state-machine transitions (update-spec-state.js), document sync (commit-docs.js), and hook behavior. These scripts were deleted, but their behavioral contracts were not migrated into equivalent shell-script tests. If similar logic is reintroduced later, there is no test baseline to port.

check-gate-2-exit.sh uses interactive read prompts, making it inherently difficult to test in CI. The fallback defaults (|| COMPILE_CONFIRM="no") provide a non-interactive path, but the actual yes/no branching logic remains unexercised in automation.

All four integration test scripts write to /tmp and to tests/reports/*.md. If executed concurrently (e.g., in a CI matrix or parallel test runner), they may collide on report files or fixture mutations (sample-feature/SPEC-STATE.md is written and deleted by test-code.sh).
testing_gaps: No automated CI invocation for tests/integration/*.sh
No test for check-gate-2-exit.sh compliance report parsing (YAML front matter vs table fallback)
No test for check-gate-2-exit.sh VERIFICATION.md generation with missing or malformed PRD.md/spec.md/tasks.md
No test for check-gate-2-exit.sh when compliance-reviewer finds BLOCK > 0
No test for check-gate-2-enter.sh when SPEC-STATE exists but is not PLAN
No test for pattern-scan.sh against an empty project directory or a directory with no Java files
test-full-flow.sh does not assert existence of skills/tech-code/SKILL.md or skills/tech-commit/SKILL.md
No test for detect-stack.sh with both pom.xml and build.gradle present (Maven priority branch)
No test for detect-stack.sh FORCE_BUILD_TOOL environment variable override
